evidence_quotes.quote: Belgiums ban on loot boxes for kids was a good step, but it's just the surface.
Games like the old GTA titles are quietly removed from sale, not because they're harmful, but because publishers fear old, good games might outshine their weaker new releases.
Players had to turn to shady links just to play again, risking malware, all because private server support wasn't allowed.
If developers enabled safe preservation tools, players wouldnt need to resort to unsafe workarounds.
payment processors like Visa have unfairly censored legal content, like anime games from Japan, even without law-breaking.
This kind of gatekeeping stifles creativity, punishes fans, and gives publishers zero reason to improve.

paragraphs.text: The Digital Fairness Act should include video games because theyve become more than just entertainment theyre cultural products, social hubs, and digital goods we deserve fair access to. Belgiums ban on loot boxes for kids was a good step, but it's just the surface. Games like the old GTA titles are quietly removed from sale, not because they're harmful, but because publishers fear old, good games might outshine their weaker new releases. Evolve is a perfect example a game left for dead by its creators, yet still loved by a loyal community. Players had to turn to shady links just to play again, risking malware, all because private server support wasn't allowed. If developers enabled safe preservation tools, players wouldnt need to resort to unsafe workarounds. On top of that, payment processors like Visa have unfairly censored legal content, like anime games from Japan, even without law-breaking. This kind of gatekeeping stifles creativity, punishes fans, and gives publishers zero reason to improve. Games deserve fairness too theyre art, history, and connection. I used a translation program so everyone can understand my message clearly.
